title: Change statuses
summary: Practice updating and deleting status fields safely in the inventory_items table. Learn to preview affected rows, use WHERE clauses, and avoid accidental data loss.

sketchBlocks.title: Why preview before changing data?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: When you update or delete rows in SQL, it's important to know exactly which records will be affected. Running a `SELECT` query with your intended `WHERE` clause lets you preview the target rows before making changes. This habit helps prevent accidental updates or deletions, especially in tables like `inventory_items` where a mistake could affect real inventory data.

sketchBlocks.title: Updating status fields
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you want to activate all draft items in the `inventory_items` table. You can preview which rows will be affected:

```sql
SELECT id, name, status FROM inventory_items WHERE status = 'draft';
```

Once you're sure, run the update:

```sql
UPDATE inventory_items SET status = 'active' WHERE status = 'draft';
```

Always use a `WHERE` clause to avoid updating every row by mistake.

sketchBlocks.title: Deleting obsolete test data
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
To clean up test items, first preview them:

```sql
SELECT id, name FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

Then, delete only those rows:

```sql
DELETE FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

Never run a `DELETE` without a `WHERE` unless you intend to remove all data!

quizDraft.title: Update all draft items to active
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL statement to update the status of all items in the inventory_items table from 'draft' to 'active'.
quizDraft.starterCode: -- Update all draft items to active
quizDraft.solutionCode: UPDATE inventory_items SET status = 'active' WHERE status = 'draft';
quizDraft.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

quizDraft.title: Delete all test items
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL statement to delete all rows from inventory_items where is_test is 1.
quizDraft.hint: Focus on the concept being tested.

help.concept: Think about the role or idea being tested rather than repeating answer wording.
help.hint_1: Eliminate choices or interpretations that do not match the task.
help.hint_2: Choose the concept that best fits what the exercise is asking you to do.

quizDraft.title: Preview items before updating
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL SELECT statement to preview all items in inventory_items that have status 'inactive'.
quizDraft.hint: Focus on the concept being tested.

help.concept: Think about the role or idea being tested rather than repeating answer wording.

quizDraft.title: Safe habits for changing data
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are good habits when updating or deleting data in SQL? Select all that apply.
options: a. Preview affected rows with a SELECT before running UPDATE or DELETE.
b. Always use a WHERE clause to target specific rows.
c. Run UPDATE or DELETE without WHERE to save time.
d. Make a backup before making major changes.
quizDraft.hint: Think about ways to avoid accidental changes.

help.concept: Previewing changes and using WHERE clauses help prevent mistakes. Backups are also important.
help.hint_1: Previewing with SELECT and using WHERE are both safe practices.
help.hint_2: Avoid running UPDATE or DELETE without WHERE, and always check which rows will be affected.

quizDraft.title: Default status value
quizDraft.prompt: What is the default value for the status column in the inventory_items table?
quizDraft.template: The default value for status is [blank].
quizDraft.hint: Check the table definition for the default value.

help.concept: The default value is set in the CREATE TABLE statement for a column.
help.hint_1: Look for DEFAULT in the schema for the status column.
help.hint_2: The status column is set to a value if none is provided during INSERT.
